text.title: Spring Boot 3.x Layered Architecture
text.summary: Project structure, layer responsibilities, and canonical package layout for Spring Boot 3.x applications.

rule.statement: Controllers handle HTTP only: deserialize request, call one service method, return response. No business logic, no repository access, no @Transactional.
rule.rationale: Controllers are HTTP adapters. Business logic in controllers cannot be tested without starting the HTTP stack.

rule.statement: Service layer owns @Transactional boundaries. Mark the class @Transactional(readOnly = true) and override individual write methods with @Transactional.
rule.rationale: Read-only transactions skip flush and allow read replicas. Write transactions need explicit opt-in to avoid accidental writes.

rule.statement: Repositories extend JpaRepository and JpaSpecificationExecutor. Custom JPQL uses @Query with named @Param. Force FetchType.LAZY on all @OneToMany, @ManyToMany, @OneToOne.
rule.rationale: Default EAGER fetch causes N+1 on collection loading. LAZY + explicit JOIN FETCH in @Query is always faster.

rule.statement: DTOs are Java records. Request records carry Bean Validation annotations; response records carry no logic. Map entity to DTO at the service boundary using MapStruct.
rule.rationale: Records are immutable and concise. Mapping at the service boundary prevents leaking JPA internals to the HTTP layer.

list.item: entity/ — X, BaseEntity (UUID id, createdAt, updatedAt, @Version)

rule.statement: Every entity extends BaseEntity with UUID @Id (GenerationType.UUID), @CreationTimestamp createdAt, @UpdateTimestamp updatedAt, and @Version for optimistic locking.
rule.rationale: UUID PKs prevent ID enumeration attacks. @Version catches concurrent modification before data corruption.

rule.statement: Use ProblemDetail (RFC 7807) in @RestControllerAdvice. Never invent custom error envelopes. Include a timestamp property on every response.
rule.rationale: RFC 7807 is the industry standard; clients have stable parsing. Custom envelopes diverge per endpoint and break frontend SDKs.
